summary: Expanded VS Records menu decompilation, especially starting the mndiagram3 module and improving matches/documentation in mndiagram and mndiagram2. The PR added the Diagram3 user-data struct and static/header declarations, implemented several page-3 routines for stat rankings, popup setup, row labels, scroll arrows, init/create/free behavior, and adjusted existing mndiagram/mndiagram2 code with matching-oriented type, stack, pointer-arithmetic, and proc-flag changes. decomp.dev reported 6 new matches and 18 unmatched-item improvements, including full matches for mnDiagram3_80247008, mnDiagram3_80246F2C, fn_80246E64, fn_80246E04, mnDiagram_PopupInputProc, and mnDiagram2_PopulateStatRows.
what_changed: Added a forward declaration for struct Diagram3 in src/melee/mn/forward.h.

Added struct Diagram3 to src/melee/mn/types.h as user data for VS Records page 3, with documented 0x78-byte layout including saved_menu, saved_selection, scroll_offset, anim_state, is_name_mode, jobjs[10], row_labels[10], title_text, value_text, row_icons[5], and popup_gobj.
Expanded src/melee/mn/mndiagram3.c from a tiny stub/free function into a substantial partial module implementation with includes for baselib gobj/jobj/sislib, ft, gm, lb, and mn dependencies.
Implemented or exposed mndiagram3 routines including mnDiagram3_80245BA4, mnDiagram3_80246D40, fn_80246E04, fn_80246E64, mnDiagram3_80246F2C, mnDiagram3_80247008, and mnDiagram3_8024714C.
Added src/melee/mn/mndiagram3.static.h with externs for mndiagram3 animation tables, data blobs, archive data, global GObj pointers, strings, GXColor, and f32/f64 constants.
Updated src/melee/mn/mndiagram3.h to include placeholder, baselib forward/gobj, and melee/mn/types.h, and to declare the newly implemented mndiagram3 functions with address comments.
Changed mnDiagram_803EE728 in mndiagram.c from Vec3[3] to Point3d[3], then used byte-based base pointers and explicit Point3d casts in mnDiagram_PopupAnimProc.

Changed mnDiagram_PopupInputProc and mnDiagram_InputProc to use Menu_GetAllInputs returning u64, cast to u32 for button tests, capture the HSD_GObjProc returned by HSD_GObjProc_8038FD54, and assign proc->flags_3 = HSD_GObj_804D783C.

Improved mnDiagram_PopupAnimProc matching by introducing HSD_Text* text, char* base, Point3d pos, f32 anim_frame, PAD_STACK(24), explicit offset casts, default_alignment writes, and an anim_frame end check that kills HSD_GObj_804D7838's proc.
Adjusted mnDiagram_80241E78 signature from s32 arg3 to int arg3 in both source and header and reordered locals/assignments for matching.
In mndiagram2, changed mnDiagram2_UpdateHeader to call mnDiagram_GetNameByIndex_s or mnDiagram_GetFighterByIndex_s directly instead of mnDiagram_GetEntityByIndex.

In mndiagram2 input/create/ranking/stat-row code, changed several local types and casts, added PAD_STACKs, used explicit writes into mn_804A04F0.buttons, and rewrote loop/control-flow shapes for better matching.
Changed mnDiagram2_InitUserData signature to accept an unused int parameter and updated the call in mnDiagram2_Create.
Changed mndiagram2 static declarations, including mnDiagram2_803EEAD0 as a u8 array and corrected/string-sized externs and GXColor extern naming.
Made small formatting/matching-oriented changes in mnvibration.c, including explicit pointer arithmetic for HSD_PadCopyStatus indexing and formatting around HSD_GObjObject_80390A70.
smart_moves: Introduced Diagram3 as a typed user-data struct before expanding page-3 functions, giving later decomp work a concrete layout for jobj/text/popup fields instead of relying on raw offsets.
Used static headers for mndiagram3 data and constants, separating module-local extern knowledge from public function declarations.
Preserved address comments in headers for newly declared routines, which keeps symbols searchable by RAM address and function name during incremental decomp.

Used byte-pointer bases such as char* base or u8* base for packed data blobs where the original code indexes mixed structs/constants by offsets; this helped match uses like base + 0x9C, base + 0x24, base + 0x30, and base + 0x64.
Captured GObjProc return values and assigned flags_3 after scheduling input/animation procs, matching the engine's expected proc state setup.
Added explicit casts and local-width changes, such as casting Menu_GetAllInputs to u32 for button checks and casting indexed fighter/name values back to u8 at call boundaries.
Applied targeted PAD_STACK and dont_inline pragmas where required by the compiler shape rather than broadly perturbing functions.
Rewrote stat ranking/list generation loops in terms of scroll offsets, limits, wraparound, and explicit temporary variables, preserving menu behavior while improving generated code similarity.

Reused existing mndiagram2 stat helpers from mndiagram3, including mnDiagram2_IsIconOnlyStat, mnDiagram2_GetRankedName, mnDiagram2_GetRankedFighter, mnDiagram2_GetAggregatedFighterRank, mnDiagram2_GetStatValue, and stat category helpers for time/distance/percentage.
decomp_lessons: For menu data blobs with mixed payloads, declaring an object as a byte array or taking a char*/u8* base can be more match-friendly than forcing a single high-level struct too early.
Point3d versus Vec3 matters for calls like lb_8000B1CC; matching may require aligning the data declaration type with the callee's expected point type even when the fields look identical.
GObjProc scheduling often needs the returned proc saved so flags_3 can be set immediately; ignoring the return may miss important codegen and behavior.
When an input helper returns a wider value than the original button tests use, preserve the wide local but cast the bit-test expression to the narrower width if that matches assembly.
Struct layout documentation in types.h is valuable once enough field offsets are known; this PR documents Diagram3 size and offsets, enabling cleaner future page-3 work.
Small signedness and width choices, such as int versus u8/u32 locals, can materially affect matching in loops and table indexing.
For init functions passed through callbacks or called from creators with unused arguments, accepting the unused parameter can be necessary to reproduce the original call shape.
Selection/ranking menu code often uses wraparound over different limits for fighter mode versus name mode; preserving the exact 0x15/0x18 thresholds is important.
Static extern sizes for sdata strings should be specific when known, e.g. char[7] and char[5], rather than generic SDATA char arrays, because symbol size and placement can influence data matching.
Matching improvements may come with small regressions elsewhere; the bot reported two unmatched-item regressions, so future changes should re-check mnDiagram_80242C0C and mndiagram2 .sdata2.
naming_conventions: Module functions keep the mnDiagram, mnDiagram2, and mnDiagram3 prefixes with address-suffixed names where semantic names are not yet known.

Known semantic helpers use descriptive names such as mnDiagram_CountUnlockedFighters, mnDiagram_PopupInputProc, mnDiagram_InputProc, mnDiagram_PopupAnimProc, mnDiagram2_UpdateHeader, mnDiagram2_CreateStatRow, mnDiagram2_PopulateStatRows, mnDiagram2_UpdateScrollArrows, and mnDiagram2_InitUserData.
Temporary unnamed functions in mndiagram3 remain fn_80246E04, fn_80246E64, fn_80246F0C, and fn_802461BC rather than inventing unproven names.
Static data symbols retain address-based names such as mnDiagram3_803EEC10, mnDiagram3_804D4FD8, mnDiagram3_804DC00C, and mnDiagram2_803EEAD0.
Struct type names follow menu page numbering: Diagram, Diagram2, and Diagram3.
Header declarations include address comments like /* 245BA4 */ before function prototypes.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: PAD_STACK was used in functions including mnDiagram_PopupAnimProc, mnDiagram2_HandleInput, mnDiagram2_GetRankedFighter, and mndiagram3 routines to tune stack layout.
Local declaration order and assignment order were adjusted in several functions, such as mnDiagram_80241E78 and mnDiagram2_Create.
Pointer arithmetic was made explicit with char*/u8* bases and casts to AnimLoopSettings*, Point3d*, Vec3*, and u16* at exact offsets.
The PR used explicit s32/u32/u8/int casts to influence sign extension, truncation, and compare patterns.
mnDiagram2_HandleInput wrote to mn_804A04F0.buttons through s32 pointer casts, likely to match paired word stores or the original memory layout.
Selection/ranking loops were reshaped as do-while loops and explicit pointer increments/shifts in mndiagram2 ranking functions.
For HSD_PadCopyStatus indexing in mnvibration.c, the code was rewritten as explicit base + index * 0x44 + 0x41 pointer arithmetic to force the desired mulli pattern.
GObj/JObj setup in mnDiagram3_80247008 follows the standard create/load-joint/object/gxlink/add-anim/req-anim/anim/userdata/proc sequence.
mndiagram3 row creation used repeated assert patterns on required JObjs, explicit JObj matrix dirty calls after translation writes, and child attachment via HSD_JObjAddChild.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR body says it was still WIP and describes the intent as more matches plus documentation in mndiagram and mndiagram2, and starting mndiagram3.
decomp.dev reported matched code increased to 53.93% (+0.03%, +1144 bytes).
decomp.dev reported 6 new matches: mnDiagram3_80247008, mnDiagram3_80246F2C, fn_80246E64, fn_80246E04, mnDiagram_PopupInputProc, and mnDiagram2_PopulateStatRows.
decomp.dev reported 18 improvements in unmatched items, with large gains in mnDiagram3_80245BA4 and mnDiagram3_8024714C and additional improvements in mndiagram and mndiagram2 functions.
decomp.dev also reported 2 regressions in unmatched items: mnDiagram_80242C0C and mndiagram2 .sdata2.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename mndiagram3 address-based functions to semantic names.
Check whether mnDiagram_80242C0C regression from PR2141 was recovered in a follow-up PR.
Search for further refinements to Diagram3 layout after this initial 0x78-byte struct.
Look for PRs that replace mndiagram3 static byte arrays with typed data structures.
Investigate whether fn_802461BC and other mndiagram3 procs were matched or named later.
Search for similar Menu_GetAllInputs u64-to-u32 casting patterns in other menu modules.
